evals.prompt: I want to run two Claude sessions at the same time — one working on a new auth feature and one fixing a bug in the API. How do I set that up without them conflicting?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step instructions for creating two git worktrees with separate branches, briefing each agent session with working directory and scope, and ensuring they work in isolation

assertions.text: Response includes the `git worktree add` command
Response creates or names two distinct branches (one for auth, one for bug/fix)
assertions.criteria: The response recommends using two separate branch names — one for the auth feature and one for the API bug fix

assertions.text: Response explains how to scope each agent to its own worktree directory
assertions.criteria: The response explains that each agent session should be pointed at (or scoped to) its own worktree working directory, so it doesn't see or touch the other session's files

assertions.text: Response conveys the one-agent-one-worktree-one-branch rule or equivalent isolation advice
assertions.criteria: The response makes clear that the two sessions should be isolated — they should not share a branch or working directory

evals.prompt: I'm in a worktree on branch feat/payments and I've finished my changes. Walk me through pushing this and opening a PR on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: Clear instructions to push the branch with -u flag and use gh pr create with appropriate flags, title format, and PR body

assertions.text: Response includes `git push` targeting the feat/payments branch
Response includes a `--title` flag or advises on a PR title format
assertions.criteria: The response includes the --title flag for gh pr create, or explains what the PR title should look like

assertions.text: Response includes a `--body` flag or advises on PR description content
assertions.criteria: The response mentions a --body flag or otherwise addresses what to put in the PR description

evals.prompt: I have three agent sessions running in parallel worktrees. Two of them both touch routes.ts — what do I do to avoid a merge conflict disaster?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of file-level isolation strategy: assign routes.ts to only one session, or sequence the conflicting sessions, with concrete options

assertions.text: Response recommends assigning routes.ts to only one session or otherwise isolating who owns it
assertions.criteria: The response advises that only one agent session should modify routes.ts, or that the sessions touching it should be sequenced rather than run in parallel

assertions.text: Response gives at least one concrete, actionable option
assertions.criteria: The response provides at least one specific action the user can take (e.g., reassign routes.ts to one session, stop one session and sequence it, or extract the routes.ts changes into a shared branch)

assertions.text: Response mentions rebase or merge ordering, or sequencing sessions
assertions.criteria: The response mentions either rebasing/merging in a specific order, or sequencing one session after the other, as a way to manage the conflict
